talk_metadata.talk_title: Transforming Cancer Surveillance to Drive Prevention and Health Equity
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "We regularly publish nine facts and figures in the statistics article on a regular basis"
Speaker said: "Our findings were used in an amicus brief in support of the Affordable Care Act in federal courts, including the Supreme Court"
Speaker said: "The data will speak in a few years"

key_data_gaps: No integration of genomic/clinical genetic data with registry information
Treatment data lag of 4-5 years limits real‑time policy assessment

crispro_opportunity.description: Linking population‑based cancer registry data with germline and somatic genomic profiles to identify high‑risk subpopulations for targeted prevention.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker noted: "If we were to have to augment the data with more clinical genetic data, probably we can do more"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can integrate large epidemiologic datasets with genomic annotations to generate predictive risk models and uncover actionable biomarkers.
external_validation_needed: PubMed: studies linking SEER data with TCGA genomic data
ClinicalTrials.gov: trials using registry‑linked genomics for prevention interventions

crispro_opportunity.description: Developing real‑time surveillance endpoints to evaluate policy impacts, reducing the 4‑5 year lag in treatment data.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "The most recent data that we have on treatment… is four, five years behind"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO’s analytics platform can process near‑real‑time claims and electronic health record feeds to generate up‑to‑date outcome metrics.
external_validation_needed: Claims databases: evaluate timeliness of treatment capture
Policy impact studies: compare lagged vs real‑time metrics

unresolved_questions: How can cancer registries be linked to genomic datasets while preserving privacy?
What methods can reduce the lag in treatment data to enable timely policy evaluation?
